description:
Load the crystal structure of bovine pancreatic ribonuclease A (PDB ID: 7RSA) into PyMOL. Your task is to perform a stereochemical analysis to identify functionally critical cis-proline residues.

1. Identify all Proline residues in the structure.
2. Calculate the omega (ω) dihedral angle for the peptide bond preceding each proline. (Recall that the omega angle is defined by the four atoms: Cα(i-1) – C(i-1) – N(i) – Cα(i)).
3. Determine which prolines are in the *cis* conformation (omega angle near 0° rather than ~180°).
4. Create a text report containing ONLY the sequence numbers of the *cis*-prolines and their calculated omega angles (do not list the trans-prolines).
5. Set up a visualization showing residues 92 and 93 in stick representation, centered and zoomed to clearly show the *cis* peptide bond.
6. Save the visualization as a high-quality PNG figure to `/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/cis_pro93.png`.
7. Save your text report to `/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/cis_proline_report.txt`.
